arts.ttl: Lothar Matthäus says Bayern Munich should extend Leon Goretzka
arts.sum: Also, be sure to stay tuned to Bavarian Podcast Works for all of your up to date coverage on Bayern Munich and Germany. “You need these aggressive players like Goretzka and Laimer in your team — not just artists like Wirtz and Musiala, but also those who do the dirty work at the back and win duels.”

arts.ttl: “Our players should stop complaining!” — Bayern Munich’s Rummenigge takes a hard stance on the congested schedule
arts.sum: Players at top clubs rarely get any breaks anymore, consistently burdened with a game every three days and seeing new competitions, such as UEFA’s Nations League and FIFA’s Club World Cup, added to the schedule. Then the money will have to come from other competitions like the new Club World Cup in the summer.

arts.ttl: Report: Bayern Munich wanted to sell Mathys Tel in order to have cash for summer transfers
arts.sum: Bayern were ready to sell Mathys Tel to Tottenham for €60m in January in order to add much needed funds to their ‘fixed deposit account’ with a view to summer transfers. According to a report from Sport Bild journalists Christian Falk and Tobi Altschäffl (as captured by @MiaSanMia ), Bayern Munich had a vested intertest in trying to sell Mathys Tel during the winter transfer window .

arts.ttl: Bayern Munich still have not given up on Alexander Nübel becoming their #1
arts.sum: Nübel will have three upcoming big tests where Bayern will be watching him closely: Friday’s Stuttgart vs Bayern, as well as the two Germany vs Italy games next month, where he’s expected to start since [competing goalkeeper Oliver] Baumann is injured. Bayern bosses have yet to rule Alexander Nübel out as a potential successor to Manuel Neuer.

arts.ttl: Arsenal sent new Joshua Kimmich message as firm answer given on Bayern contract talks
arts.sum: Joshua Kimmich has emerged as a top target for Arsenal as his contract negotiations with Bayern Munich continue to stall, with further talks having taken place over the weekend

arts.ttl: Rumours linking Juventus star Kenan Yildiz to a Bayern Munich return are false
arts.sum: Recent rumours linking Juventus winger Kenan Yildiz to Bayern Munich have been revealed to be false. Reports have since emerged stating that Bayern has interest in bringing the player back to the club with a fee being negotiated in the 60 to 90 million Euro range, a rumour that has now been quashed.
